Article.ArticleTitle: Natural deep eutectic solvent treatment for sludge deep dewatering with simultaneous enhancement of protein release at ambient temperature: Effect of tannic acid assistance.

Abstract.AbstractText: Although the combination of natural deep eutectic solvents (NADES) with heating treatment significantly improves the dewatering performance of waste activated sludge (WAS), widespread practical application remains limited due to the substantial energy input required. To address this issue, this study introduced an innovative conditioning method using oxalic acid-based NADES (OA-NADES)/tannic acid (TA) to treat sludge. At ambient temperature, OA-NADES/TA treatment of sludge reduced the cake water content (Wc) and capillary suction time (CST) by 31.22 % and 80.78 %, respectively. Simultaneously, protein release increased by 65.28-fold. Mechanistic studies have shown that TA enhanced the destructive effect of OA-NADES on sludge structure to release biopolymers inside extracellular polymeric substances (EPS). Meanwhile, OA-NADES enhanced the binding sites between TA and hydrophilic proteins. This promoted the formation of additional π-π stacked hydrophobic bonds between TA and EPS. In turn, it facilitated the assembly of protein-polyphenol complexes. These complexes further disrupted the secondary structure of the proteins, thereby promoting the release of bound water. The OA-NADES/TA treatment markedly enhanced WAS dewaterability by effectively solubilizing organic matter, complexing proteins, and specifically targeting EPS. This work therefore paves the way for innovative WAS management strategies that concurrently improve dewatering and enable resource recovery.
